message:
perf: defer allow-once I/O past keyword quick-reject (20x speedup)

Move allow_once_match() from Step 1.6 (before any keyword checking) to
after the keyword quick-reject gate. Allow-once entries only exist for
commands that were previously blocked by a pattern, which means they
must have matched keywords — so deferring past quick-reject is safe.

This eliminates ~65µs of filesystem I/O (current_dir, env::var,
dirs::home_dir, path.exists) from every unrelated command evaluation.

Benchmark impact (hook_latency, keyword_rejection/fast_reject):
  Before: ~6.0µs per command (cargo, python, node, make, gcc)
  After:  ~300ns per command
  Speedup: ~20x on the quick-reject hot path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
